testcase.name: test_orphaned_services_after_shrink_cluster

failure.message: FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system.log'

testcase.failure:
src = 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system.log' dst = 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system_24_12_20_13_10_43.log' copy_function = <function copy2 at 0x745165d93a30>
def move(src, dst, copy_function=copy2): """Recursively move a file or directory to another location. This is similar to the Unix "mv" command. Return the file or directory's destination.
If the destination is a directory or a symlink to a directory, the source is moved inside the directory. The destination path must not already exist.
If the destination already exists but is not a directory, it may be overwritten depending on os.rename() semantics.
If the destination is on our current filesystem, then rename() is used. Otherwise, src is copied to the destination and then removed. Symlinks are recreated under the new name if os.rename() fails because of cross filesystem renames.
The optional `copy_function` argument is a callable that will be used to copy the source or it will be delegated to `copytree`. By default, copy2() is used, but any function that supports the same signature (like copy()) can be used.
A lot more could be done here... A look at a mv.c shows a lot of the issues this implementation glosses over.
""" sys.audit("shutil.move", src, dst) real_dst = dst if os.path.isdir(dst): if _samefile(src, dst): # We might be on a case insensitive filesystem, # perform the rename anyway. os.rename(src, dst) return
# Using _basename instead of os.path.basename is important, as we must # ignore any trailing slash to avoid the basename returning '' real_dst = os.path.join(dst, _basename(src))
if os.path.exists(real_dst): raise Error("Destination path '%s' already exists" % real_dst) try: > os.rename(src, real_dst) E FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system.log' -> 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system_24_12_20_13_10_43.log'
../../.pyenv/versions/3.10.12/lib/python3.10/shutil.py:816: FileNotFoundError
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
db_cluster = <sdcm.cluster_k8s.mini_k8s.LocalMinimalScyllaPodCluster object at 0x74514bf13bb0>
def test_orphaned_services_after_shrink_cluster(db_cluster): """ Issue https://github.com/scylladb/scylla-operator/issues/514 """ log.info("Add node to the rack 0") new_node = db_cluster.add_nodes(count=1, dc_idx=0, enable_auto_bootstrap=True, rack=0)[0] svc_name = new_node.name
log.info("Decommission newly added node from the rack 0") > db_cluster.decommission(new_node)
functional_tests/scylla_operator/test_functional.py:583: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py:2904: in decommission self.terminate_node(node, scylla_shards=scylla_shards) ../../.pyenv/versions/3.10.12/lib/python3.10/shutil.py:836: in move copy_function(src, real_dst) ../../.pyenv/versions/3.10.12/lib/python3.10/shutil.py:434: in copy2 copyfile(src, dst, follow_symlinks=follow_symlinks) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
src = 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system.log' dst = 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system_24_12_20_13_10_43.log'
def copyfile(src, dst, *, follow_symlinks=True): """Copy data from src to dst in the most efficient way possible.
If follow_symlinks is not set and src is a symbolic link, a new symlink will be created instead of copying the file it points to.
""" sys.audit("shutil.copyfile", src, dst)
if _samefile(src, dst): raise SameFileError("{!r} and {!r} are the same file".format(src, dst))
file_size = 0 for i, fn in enumerate([src, dst]): try: st = _stat(fn) except OSError: # File most likely does not exist pass else: # XXX What about other special files? (sockets, devices...) if stat.S_ISFIFO(st.st_mode): fn = fn.path if isinstance(fn, os.DirEntry) else fn raise SpecialFileError("`%s` is a named pipe" % fn) if _WINDOWS and i == 0: file_size = st.st_size
if not follow_symlinks and _islink(src): os.symlink(os.readlink(src), dst) else: > with open(src, 'rb') as fsrc: E FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-db-cluster-acd6186a/sct-cluster-local-dc-1-rack-1-3/system.log'
../../.pyenv/versions/3.10.12/lib/python3.10/shutil.py:254: FileNotFoundError

testcase.name: test_orphaned_services_after_shrink_cluster

error.message: failed on teardown with "Failed: Failed to bring cluster nodes back to original state due to : Traceback (most recent call last): File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/functional_tests/scylla_operator/conftest.py", line 138, in _bring_cluster_back_to_original_state current_cluster_spec = db_cluster.get_scylla_cluster_plain_value('/spec') File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 2609, in get_scylla_cluster_plain_value """ File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 2580, in _k8s_scylla_cluster_api def _k8s_scylla_cluster_api(self, dc_idx: int = 0) -> Resource: File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1505, in dynamic_client def dynamic_client(self) -> k8s.dynamic.DynamicClient: File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1496, in api_client def api_client(self) -> k8s.client.ApiClient: File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1501, in get_api_client return self.api_call_rate_limiter.get_api_client(self.k8s_configuration) File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1492, in k8s_configuration return self.api_call_rate_limiter.get_k8s_configuration(self) File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/utils/k8s/__init__.py", line 236, in create_k8s_configuration k8s.config.load_kube_config( File "/home/grzywink/.pyenv/versions/sct-3.10.12/lib/python3.10/site-packages/kubernetes/config/kube_config.py", line 808, in load_kube_config loader = _get_kube_config_loader( File "/home/grzywink/.pyenv/versions/sct-3.10.12/lib/python3.10/site-packages/kubernetes/config/kube_config.py", line 767, in _get_kube_config_loader raise ConfigException( kubernetes.config.config_exception.ConfigException: Invalid kube-config file. No configuration found."

testcase.error:
tester = <functional_tests.scylla_operator.libs.auxiliary.ScyllaOperatorFunctionalClusterTester testMethod=runTest> config_map = {'scylla.yaml': 'hinted_handoff_enabled: false\n'} original_scylla_cluster_spec = {'agentRepository': 'scylladb/scylla-manager-agent', 'agentVersion': '3.4.0', 'automaticOrphanedNodeCleanup': True, 'd...}, 'podAntiAffinity': {'preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution': [...]}, 'tolerations': [{...}]}, ...}]}, ...}
def _bring_cluster_back_to_original_state( tester: ScyllaOperatorFunctionalClusterTester, config_map: dict, original_scylla_cluster_spec: dict ): restart = False db_cluster = tester.db_cluster try: # Restore cluster spec, there is one problem though: # s-o does not support rack removal, so if we see an extra rack we need to remove members form it # but keep it in the cluster spec # Other alternative is to redeploy cluster, which is expensive and we will do so only if it is found needed
original_rack_specs = original_scylla_cluster_spec.get('datacenter', {}).get('racks', []) > current_cluster_spec = db_cluster.get_scylla_cluster_plain_value('/spec')
functional_tests/scylla_operator/conftest.py:138: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <sdcm.cluster_k8s.mini_k8s.LocalMinimalScyllaPodCluster object at 0x74514bf13bb0> path = '/spec', dc_idx = 0
def get_scylla_cluster_plain_value(self, path: str, dc_idx: int = 0) -> Union[Dict, List, str, None]: """ Get scylla cluster value from kubernetes API and converts result to basic python data types. Use it if you are going to modify the data. > """

@property def k8s_configuration(self) -> k8s.client.Configuration: if self.api_call_rate_limiter: > return self.api_call_rate_limiter.get_k8s_configuration(self)
sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py:1492: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
kluster = <sdcm.cluster_k8s.mini_k8s.LocalKindCluster object at 0x74514bf561a0>
@staticmethod def create_k8s_configuration(kluster) -> k8s.client.Configuration: k8s_configuration = k8s.client.Configuration() if kluster.k8s_server_url: k8s_configuration.host = kluster.k8s_server_url else: > k8s.config.load_kube_config( config_file=kluster.kube_config_path, client_configuration=k8s_configuration)
sdcm/utils/k8s/__init__.py:236: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
config_file = 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-acd6186a/.kube/config' context = None client_configuration = <kubernetes.client.configuration.Configuration object at 0x745153bff550> persist_config = True
def load_kube_config(config_file=None, context=None, client_configuration=None, persist_config=True): """Loads authentication and cluster information from kube-config file and stores them in kubernetes.client.configuration.
:param config_file: Name of the kube-config file. :param context: set the active context. If is set to None, current_context from config file will be used. :param client_configuration: The kubernetes.client.Configuration to set configs to. :param persist_config: If True, config file will be updated when changed (e.g GCP token refresh). """
if config_file is None: config_file = KUBE_CONFIG_DEFAULT_LOCATION
> loader = _get_kube_config_loader( filename=config_file, active_context=context, persist_config=persist_config)
../../.pyenv/versions/sct-3.10.12/lib/python3.10/site-packages/kubernetes/config/kube_config.py:808: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
filename = 'pwd/logs/20241220-125744-887981/functional-grzywink-acd6186a/.kube/config' config_dict = None, persist_config = True kwargs = {'active_context': None, 'config_persister': <bound method KubeConfigMerger.save_changes of <kubernetes.config.kube_config.KubeConfigMerger object at 0x745148152f50>>} kcfg = <kubernetes.config.kube_config.KubeConfigMerger object at 0x745148152f50>
def _get_kube_config_loader( filename=None, config_dict=None, persist_config=False, **kwargs): if config_dict is None: kcfg = KubeConfigMerger(filename) if persist_config and 'config_persister' not in kwargs: kwargs['config_persister'] = kcfg.save_changes
if kcfg.config is None: > raise ConfigException( 'Invalid kube-config file. ' 'No configuration found.') E kubernetes.config.config_exception.ConfigException: Invalid kube-config file. No configuration found.
../../.pyenv/versions/sct-3.10.12/lib/python3.10/site-packages/kubernetes/config/kube_config.py:767: ConfigException
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
tester = <functional_tests.scylla_operator.libs.auxiliary.ScyllaOperatorFunctionalClusterTester testMethod=runTest>
@pytest.fixture(name="db_cluster") def fixture_db_cluster(tester: ScyllaOperatorFunctionalClusterTester): if not tester.healthy_flag: pytest.skip('cluster is not healthy, skipping rest of the tests')
with tester.db_cluster.scylla_config_map() as scylla_config_map: original_scylla_config_map = scylla_config_map original_scylla_cluster_spec = tester.db_cluster.get_scylla_cluster_plain_value('/spec')
if dataset_name := tester.db_cluster.params.get("k8s_functional_test_dataset"): tester.db_cluster.wait_for_nodes_up_and_normal(nodes=tester.db_cluster.nodes, verification_node=tester.db_cluster.nodes[0]) tester.db_cluster.wait_for_init(node_list=tester.db_cluster.nodes, wait_for_db_logs=True) tester.db_cluster.prefill_cluster(dataset_name) tester.db_cluster.k8s_cluster = tester.db_cluster.k8s_clusters[0] yield tester.db_cluster
if tester.healthy_flag: > _bring_cluster_back_to_original_state( tester, config_map=original_scylla_config_map, original_scylla_cluster_spec=original_scylla_cluster_spec )
functional_tests/scylla_operator/conftest.py:117: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
tester = <functional_tests.scylla_operator.libs.auxiliary.ScyllaOperatorFunctionalClusterTester testMethod=runTest> config_map = {'scylla.yaml': 'hinted_handoff_enabled: false\n'} original_scylla_cluster_spec = {'agentRepository': 'scylladb/scylla-manager-agent', 'agentVersion': '3.4.0', 'automaticOrphanedNodeCleanup': True, 'd...}, 'podAntiAffinity': {'preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution': [...]}, 'tolerations': [{...}]}, ...}]}, ...}
def _bring_cluster_back_to_original_state( tester: ScyllaOperatorFunctionalClusterTester, config_map: dict, original_scylla_cluster_spec: dict ): restart = False db_cluster = tester.db_cluster try: # Restore cluster spec, there is one problem though: # s-o does not support rack removal, so if we see an extra rack we need to remove members form it # but keep it in the cluster spec # Other alternative is to redeploy cluster, which is expensive and we will do so only if it is found needed
original_rack_specs = original_scylla_cluster_spec.get('datacenter', {}).get('racks', []) current_cluster_spec = db_cluster.get_scylla_cluster_plain_value('/spec') current_rack_specs = current_cluster_spec.get('datacenter', {}).get('racks', []) if len(original_rack_specs) < len(current_rack_specs): # A new racks with 0 members in them to the original cluster specification # At this point original_rack_specs is more like cluster spec we want to have new_racks = current_rack_specs[len(original_rack_specs):] for rack in new_racks: rack['members'] = 0 original_rack_specs.extend(new_racks)
# Restore config-map scylla-config with db_cluster.scylla_config_map() as recover_config_map: if recover_config_map != config_map: recover_config_map.clear() recover_config_map.update(config_map) restart = True
# Exclude some fields from diff, since they are not important for us cluster_spec_diff = DeepDiff(original_scylla_cluster_spec, current_cluster_spec, ignore_order=True, exclude_paths=["forceRedeploymentReason", "repairs", "backups"]) if cluster_spec_diff: # If cluster spec we currently have is not equal to what we want replace it. # It will cause scylla pods rollout restart on the operator level. # WARNING: if number of nodes differs than we will have incorrect data # in "db_cluster.nodes". For the moment all changes to node number must # go though 'add_nodes' and 'decommision' methods only. LOGGER.info("Rollout original cluster state due spec change: %s", cluster_spec_diff) db_cluster.replace_scylla_cluster_value('/spec', original_scylla_cluster_spec) # sleep for 3 minutes to make sure we wait for this rollout to finish (in case some other was running), # no impact on test time as anyway rollout takes more than 3 minutes time.sleep(180) db_cluster.wait_sts_rollout_restart(len(db_cluster.nodes)) restart = False
if restart: db_cluster.restart_scylla() db_cluster.wait_for_nodes_up_and_normal( nodes=db_cluster.nodes, verification_node=db_cluster.nodes[0]) except Exception as exc: # pylint: disable=broad-except # noqa: BLE001 tester.healthy_flag = False > pytest.fail("Failed to bring cluster nodes back to original state due to :\n" + "".join(traceback.format_exception(type(exc), exc, exc.__traceback__))) E Failed: Failed to bring cluster nodes back to original state due to : E Traceback (most recent call last): E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/functional_tests/scylla_operator/conftest.py", line 138, in _bring_cluster_back_to_original_state E current_cluster_spec = db_cluster.get_scylla_cluster_plain_value('/spec') E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 2609, in get_scylla_cluster_plain_value E """ E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 2580, in _k8s_scylla_cluster_api E def _k8s_scylla_cluster_api(self, dc_idx: int = 0) -> Resource: E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1505, in dynamic_client E def dynamic_client(self) -> k8s.dynamic.DynamicClient: E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1496, in api_client E def api_client(self) -> k8s.client.ApiClient: E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1501, in get_api_client E return self.api_call_rate_limiter.get_api_client(self.k8s_configuration) E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/cluster_k8s/__init__.py", line 1492, in k8s_configuration E return self.api_call_rate_limiter.get_k8s_configuration(self) E File "/home/grzywink/Projects/scylla-cluster-tests/sdcm/utils/k8s/__init__.py", line 236, in create_k8s_configuration E k8s.config.load_kube_config( E File "/home/grzywink/.pyenv/versions/sct-3.10.12/lib/python3.10/site-packages/kubernetes/config/kube_config.py", line 808, in load_kube_config E loader = _get_kube_config_loader( E File "/home/grzywink/.pyenv/versions/sct-3.10.12/lib/python3.10/site-packages/kubernetes/config/kube_config.py", line 767, in _get_kube_config_loader E raise ConfigException( E kubernetes.config.config_exception.ConfigException: Invalid kube-config file. No configuration found.
functional_tests/scylla_operator/conftest.py:180: Failed
